first_paragraph: ***Two Crowded Hours*** is a 1931 British comedy drama film directed by [Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell") and starring [John Longden](/wiki/John_Longden "John Longden"), [Jane Welsh](/wiki/Jane_Welsh "Jane Welsh"), and [Jerry Verno](/wiki/Jerry_Verno "Jerry Verno"). It was made as a [Quota quickie](/wiki/Cinematograph_Films_Act_1927 "Cinematograph Films Act 1927") and is the first film where Powell is credited as the director. No known copies of the film have survived to the present day, and *Two Crowded Hours* has been declared to be "Missing, Believed Lost" by the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

1931 film

***Two Crowded Hours*** is a 1931 British comedy drama film directed by [Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell") and starring [John Longden](/wiki/John_Longden "John Longden"), [Jane Welsh](/wiki/Jane_Welsh "Jane Welsh"), and [Jerry Verno](/wiki/Jerry_Verno "Jerry Verno"). It was made as a [Quota quickie](/wiki/Cinematograph_Films_Act_1927 "Cinematograph Films Act 1927") and is the first film where Powell is credited as the director. No known copies of the film have survived to the present day, and *Two Crowded Hours* has been declared to be "Missing, Believed Lost" by the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Premise

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Two_Crowded_Hours&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Premise")]

A murderer is on the run from prison and is out to get everyone who put him there, especially Joyce, a young woman. Detective Fielding gives chase with the help of a London [cabbie](/wiki/Taxi "Taxi"), Jim, who has aspirations of becoming a policeman himself.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Two_Crowded_Hours&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [John Longden](/wiki/John_Longden "John Longden") as Harry Fielding
* [Jane Welsh](/wiki/Jane_Welsh "Jane Welsh") as Joyce Danton
* [Jerry Verno](/wiki/Jerry_Verno "Jerry Verno") as Jim
* [Michael Hogan](/wiki/Michael_Hogan_(British_actor) "Michael Hogan (British actor)") as Scammell
* Edward Barber as Tom Murray

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Two_Crowded_Hours&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

[Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell")'s first feature as director, *Two Crowded Hours* was produced by [Jerry Jackson](/wiki/Jerome_Jackson_(producer) "Jerome Jackson (producer)") for the Film Engineering Company and distributed by the British arm of Fox Pictures. With accomplished players [John Longden](/wiki/John_Longden "John Longden") (star of *[Blackmail](/wiki/Blackmail_(1929_film) "Blackmail (1929 film)")*) and Cockney character actor [Jerry Verno](/wiki/Jerry_Verno "Jerry Verno"), shooting was completed in 12 days in April 1931 in and around [London](/wiki/London "London")'s [Soho](/wiki/Soho "Soho"). "It was played for laughs and thrills", Powell said, "and we were paid £1 per foot by Fox. We got £4,000 on delivery so obviously we had to make it for £3,000".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Status

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Two_Crowded_Hours&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Status")]

Although a few stills survive, there is no known print of *Two Crowded Hours* in existence*. Two Crowded Hours* has therefore been declared to be "Missing, Believed Lost" by the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It is listed as one of their "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" lost films, along with two later Powell films *[The Price of a Song](/wiki/The_Price_of_a_Song "The Price of a Song")* (1935) and *[The Man Behind the Mask](/wiki/The_Man_Behind_the_Mask "The Man Behind the Mask")* (1936).[[4]](#cite_note-4)
